html:
[2224]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 无礼 - A term indicating a lack of courtesy or respect.
  • 下马 - To dismount from a horse.
  • 失辔 - To lose one's grip on the reins.
  • 拜舞 - To bow and dance, a form of respectful greeting.
  • 拢马 - To guide or control a horse.
  • 防侍 - To guard and attend.
  • 太上皇 - The title given to the retired emperor.
  • 诰 - An imperial decree.
  • 矫诏 - To issue a false or altered imperial decree.
  • 大渐 - A term indicating a serious illness.
  • 专朝 - To hold exclusive power in the court.
  • 复有变故 - To have another incident or change.
  • 应科目 - To take part in the imperial examinations.
  • 主持 - To support or advocate for someone.
  • 朝对 - To appear before the emperor.
  • 羸弱 - Weak or frail.
  • 致政 - To retire from office.
  • 授 - To be appointed or assigned a position.
  • 朝天 - To ascend to the imperial court.
  • 倒地落 - To fall to the ground.
  • 定也 - To be decided or determined.
  • 清俭文学 - Clean, frugal, and literary.
  • 贤相 - A virtuous and capable minister.
  • 毁滥 - To slander or defame.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 李辅国 - A powerful eunuch during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 肃宗 - Emperor Suzong of Tang, the third emperor of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 太上皇 - The title given to Emperor Xuanzong of Tang after he abdicated in favor of his son, Emperor Suzong.
  • 西内 - The western palace, a specific location within the imperial palace complex.
  • 力士 - A strong man or warrior.
  • 长流远恶 - To be exiled to a distant and harsh place.
  • 度支 - The department responsible for financial affairs in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 京兆尹 - The governor of the capital region.
  • 嗣道王实 - A person mentioned in the text, likely a high-ranking official.
  • 刘师老 - A person mentioned in the text, likely a scholar or official.
  • 穆寂 - A person mentioned in the text, likely a scholar or official.
  • 郑絪 - A prominent official and scholar during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 昭国司徒公 - A title given to a high-ranking official, likely referring to Vizier Zhaoguo.
  • 嘉话录 - A historical record or collection of anecdotes.

Proper Nouns

  • 李辅国 - Li Fuguo, a powerful eunuch during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 肃宗 - Emperor Suzong of Tang, the third emperor of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 太上皇 - The title given to Emperor Xuanzong of Tang after he abdicated.
  • 西内 - The western palace, a specific location within the imperial palace complex.
  • 力士 - A strong man or warrior.
  • 长流远恶 - Exile to a distant and harsh place.
  • 度支 - The department responsible for financial affairs in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 京兆尹 - The governor of the capital region.
  • 嗣道王实 - A high-ranking official.
  • 刘师老 - A scholar or official.
  • 穆寂 - A scholar or official.
  • 郑絪 - A prominent official and scholar during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 昭国司徒公 - A title given to a high-ranking official, likely referring to Vizier Zhaoguo.
  • 嘉话录 - A historical record or collection of anecdotes.
  • 贞元 - The era name of Emperor Dezong of Tang.
  • 太府卿 - The head of the Ministry of Finance.
  • 金吾 - The head of the imperial guards.
  • 度支 - The department responsible for financial affairs.
  • 京兆尹 - The governor of the capital region.
  • 韦渠牟 - Wei Qumu, a high-ranking official.
  • 李齐运 - Li Qiyun, a high-ranking official.
  • 裴延龄 - Pei Yanling, a high-ranking official.
  • 嗣道王实 - Wang Shi, a high-ranking official.
  • 刘师老 - Liu Shilao, a scholar or official.
  • 穆寂 - Mu Ji, a scholar or official.
  • 郑絪 - Zheng Yin, a prominent official and scholar during the Tang Dynasty.

[2225]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 魚朝恩專權使氣 - Monopolized power and acted arrogantly.
  • 公卿不敢仰視 - Ministers dared not look up to him.
  • 宰臣或決政事不預謀者 - Ministers who decided on political matters without consulting others.
  • 睚眦曰 - He would say with a glare.
  • 天下之事豈不由我乎 - How can the affairs of the world not be decided by me?
  • 朝恩幼子令徽年十四五 - Yu Chao'en's young son, Linghui, was around 14 or 15 years old.
  • 始給事於內殿 - He began serving in the inner palace.
  • 特賜綠 - He was specially granted the rank of green.
  • 未浃旬月 - Within a few days or months.
  • 同列黄門位居令徽上者 - Those of the same rank who were above Linghui in position.
  • 因叙立於殿前 - They lined up in order in front of the hall.
  • 恐其后至 - Fearing that they would come later.
  • 爭路以進 - They competed to advance.
  • 無何誤觸令徽臂 - Soon they accidentally bumped into Linghui's arm.
  • 班次居下為同列所欺 - That he was placed below in rank and was being deceived by his peers.
  • 願陛下特賜金章以超其等 - He wished that the emperor would specially grant him a golden seal to elevate his rank.
  • 不言其绯而便求紫 - Without mentioning his red rank, he immediately requested a purple rank.
  • 鱼氏在朝动无畏惮他皆仿此 - The Yu family in court always acted without fear, and others followed suit.
  • 同列黄门寻逐于岭表 - Those of the same rank were soon expelled to the southern frontier.
  • 及朝恩被杀天下无不快焉 - When Yu Chao'en was killed, everyone in the world rejoiced.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鱼朝恩 - A powerful eunuch during the Tang Dynasty, known for his influence and arrogance.
  • 公卿 - Refers to high-ranking officials in the imperial court.
  • 宰臣 - Refers to the prime minister or chief minister.
  • 内殿 - Refers to the inner palace, where the emperor and his close advisors met.
  • 黄门 - Refers to eunuchs who served in the imperial court.
  • 金章 - A golden seal, a symbol of high rank and authority.
  • 紫衣 - Purple robes, a symbol of high rank and nobility.
  • 绯 - Red robes, a symbol of lower rank.
  • 岭表 - Refers to the southern frontier of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 杜阳杂编 - A historical text from the Tang Dynasty, which records various anecdotes and stories.

[2226]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 元載在中書有丈人自宣州貨所居來投求一職事 - Describes a man from Xuanchou selling his property and seeking a position at the Ministry of Central Affairs; elliptical structure requires careful unpacking.
  • 中書度其人材不任职事赠河北一函书而遣之 - Compounds the actions of assessing the man's abilities, finding him unsuitable, and sending him away with a letter to Hebei.
  • 丈人惋怒不得已持书而去 - Describes the man's reaction: angry and disappointed but forced to take the letter and leave.
  • 既至幽州念破产而来止得一书书若恳切犹可望 - Upon arriving in Youzhou, the man reflects on selling his property and receiving only one letter, which, if sincere, might still be useful.
  • 书既入馆之上舍留连积月及辞去奉绢一千匹 - After delivering the letter, the man stays for several months and is given a thousand bolts of silk before leaving.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 元載 - Refers to Yuan Zai, a prominent official during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 中書 - Refers to the Ministry of Central Affairs, one of the six ministries in the Tang government.
  • 丈人 - Refers to a man, often used as a respectful term for an older man.
  • 宣州 - An ancient Chinese prefecture, now part of modern-day Anhui Province.
  • 河北 - Refers to the region north of the Yellow River, a significant area during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 幽州 - An ancient Chinese commandery, located in what is now Beijing.
  • 幽闲鼓吹 - The title of a historical novel, suggesting this story is part of a larger narrative or collection of anecdotes.

Proper Nouns

  • 元載 - Yuan Zai, a Tang Dynasty official.
  • 中書 - The Ministry of Central Affairs.
  • 宣州 - An ancient Chinese prefecture.
  • 河北 - A region north of the Yellow River.
  • 幽州 - An ancient Chinese commandery.
  • 幽闲鼓吹 - The title of a historical novel.

[2227]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 勢傾中外 - Indicates immense power and influence both inside and outside the imperial court.
  • 恣為瓦石所擊 - Describes being attacked by stones and tiles.
  • 舊例宰相出鎭府司無例發人防守 - Means that it was customary not to send people to guard the governor when a chancellor left for his post.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 元載 - A powerful chancellor during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 福州觀察使 - The governor of Fuzhou, a prefecture in Fujian.
  • 康崑崙 - A famous pipa player during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 叚和尚 - A monk known for his skill in playing the pipa.
  • 梁州 - A musical tune.
  • 路岩 - A chancellor during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 溫璋 - A former governor of the capital.
  • 薛能 - A provincial inspector.
  • 李蠙 - A person who made a decision.
  • 懿宗 - Emperor Yizong of Tang.
  • 淮南 - A region in southern China.
  • 崔 - A person who worked under Lu Yan.
  • 翰林 - The Hanlin Academy, a prestigious institution in the Tang Dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 元載 - Yuan Zai
  • 福州觀察使 - Governor of Fuzhou
  • 康崑崙 - Kang Kunlun
  • 叚和尚 - Duan Monk
  • 梁州 - Liangzhou
  • 路岩 - Lu Yan
  • 溫璋 - Wen Zhang
  • 薛能 - Xue Neng
  • 李蠙 - Li Bing
  • 懿宗 - Emperor Yizong
  • 淮南 - Huainan
  • 崔 - Cui Shu
  • 翰林 - Hanlin Academy

[2228]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 元和初黜八司马 - Refers to the early Yuanhe period when the 'Eight Chancellors' were demoted.
  • 韦执谊崖州韩泰防州桞宗元永州刘禹锡朗州韩饶州凌凖连州程异栁州 - Lists the officials and their respective demotions to various states.
  • 咸通韦保衡路岩作相除不附已者十司戸崔沆循州李渎绣州萧遘播州崔彦融雷州高湘髙州张顔潘州李贶勤州杜裔休端州郑彦持义州李藻费州内绣州潘州雷州三人不廻 - Describes the actions of Weibo and Lu Yan during the Shatong period, where they removed officials who were not aligned with them.
  • 高湜与弟湘少不相睦 - Indicates that Gao Shì and his brother Gao Xiang had a strained relationship from a young age.
  • 咸通末既出高州湜雅与路岩相善见岩阳救湘岩曰某与舍人皆是京兆府荷枷者先是刘瞻志欲除岩温璋希防别制新枷数十待之瞻以人情附已不甚缄密其计泄焉故居岩之后湜既知举问岩所欲言时岩以去年停举已潜奏恐有遗滞请加十人矣即托湜以五人湜喜其数寡形于言色不累日十人制下湜未知之也岩执诏笑谓湜曰前者五人侍郎所惠也今之十人某自致也湜竟依其数放焉湘到任嗔湜不佑已尝赋诗云唯有髙州是当家 - This lengthy passage describes the complex interactions and political maneuvers involving Gao Shì, Lu Yan, and others, including the exchange of favors and the eventual outcome of Gao Xiang's appointment.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 元和 - The early period of the Tang Dynasty, specifically the reign of Emperor Xianzong.
  • 咸通 - The middle period of the Tang Dynasty, specifically the reign of Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 韦执谊 - A prominent official who was demoted during the Yuanhe period.
  • 韩泰 - Another official who was demoted during the Yuanhe period.
  • 刘禹锡 - A renowned poet and official who was demoted during the Yuanhe period.
  • 韦保衡 - A powerful official during the Shatong period.
  • 路岩 - Another influential official during the Shatong period.
  • 京兆府 - The capital city of Chang'an, where many officials were based.
  • 荷枷者 - Refers to officials who were under scrutiny or punishment.
  • 刘瞻 - An official who attempted to remove Weibo and Lu Yan.
  • 温璋 - An official who supported Duan Zhan's efforts to remove Weibo and Lu Yan.
  • 萧遘 - An official who was demoted during the Shatong period.
  • 播州 - A state where officials were demoted.
  • 绣州 - A state where officials were demoted.
  • 雷州 - A state where officials were demoted.
  • 高州 - A state where officials were demoted.
  • 潘州 - A state where officials were demoted.
  • 费州 - A state where officials were demoted.
  • 义州 - A state where officials were demoted.
  • 端州 - A state where officials were demoted.
  • 郑彦持 - An official who was demoted during the Shatong period.
  • 李藻 - An official who was demoted during the Shatong period.
  • 杜裔休 - An official who was demoted during the Shatong period.
  • 李贶 - An official who was demoted during the Shatong period.
  • 张顔 - An official who was demoted during the Shatong period.
  • 程异 - An official who was demoted during the Shatong period.
  • 凌凖 - An official who was demoted during the Yuanhe period.

[2229]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 黜辱者数矣 - Repeatedly disgraced and humiliated, referring to the frequent rejection and humiliation of Lu Yin and Li Qiao by Wang Duo.
  • 特除右司员外郎 - Especially appointed as a Right Counselor of the Right, indicating a special favor or exception.
  • 右丞崔沆不听 - The Right Chancellor Cui Hang did not agree, showing resistance to the appointment.
  • 隐上省仍即见携于私第 - Lu Yin went to the government office and then visited Li Xie at his private residence, indicating a personal meeting.
  • 舍弟极屈即当上陈 - My younger brother is extremely wronged, and I will immediately report it to the authorities, showing Li Xie's frustration and intention to appeal.
  • 既上沆乃求假 - After the report was submitted, Cui Hang then requested leave, indicating a response to the report.
  • 携即时替沆官 - Li Xie immediately replaced Cui Hang's position, showing a swift change in office.
  • 吾见丞郎出省郎未见省郎出丞郎 - I have seen a counselor leave, but I have not seen a provincial officer leave a counselor, implying a reversal of roles or a significant event.
  • 諌官似狗一个吠辄一时有声 - The censors are like dogs; one barks and they all bark at once, using a metaphor to criticize the behavior of censors.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 卢隐李峭皆滑帅王铎之门生 - Lu Yin and Li Qiao were both disciples of the governor of Huazhou, Wang Duo, indicating their relationship to a powerful figure.
  • 隐以从兄携为相特除右司员外郎 - Lu Yin, through his elder brother Li Xie who was the chancellor, was specially appointed as a Right Counselor of the Right, indicating a family connection and political maneuvering.
  • 右丞崔沆不听 - The Right Chancellor Cui Hang did not agree, showing political opposition.
  • 隐上省仍即见携于私第 - Lu Yin went to the government office and then visited Li Xie at his private residence, indicating a personal and political strategy.
  • 舍弟极屈即当上陈 - My younger brother is extremely wronged, and I will immediately report it to the authorities, showing a family's attempt to seek justice.
  • 既上沆乃求假 - After the report was submitted, Cui Hang then requested leave, indicating a possible reaction to the report.
  • 携即时替沆官 - Li Xie immediately replaced Cui Hang's position, showing a significant political change.
  • 吾见丞郎出省郎未见省郎出丞郎 - I have seen a counselor leave, but I have not seen a provincial officer leave a counselor, implying a reversal of roles or a significant event.
  • 諌官似狗一个吠辄一时有声 - The censors are like dogs; one barks and they all bark at once, using a metaphor to criticize the behavior of censors.

Proper Nouns

  • 卢隐 - A person mentioned in the text.
  • 李峭 - A person mentioned in the text.
  • 王铎 - The governor of Huazhou.
  • 从兄携 - Lu Yin's elder brother and the chancellor.
  • 崔沆 - The Right Chancellor.
  • 諌官 - Officials responsible for advising the emperor.

[2230]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 善撫士卒 - Means 'good at treating soldiers kindly.'
  • 輕士大夫 - Means 'dislikes or undervalues literati and officials.'
  • 敬禮士大夫 - Means 'respects and treats literati and officials with courtesy.'
  • 輕卒伍 - Means 'dislikes or undervalues common soldiers.'
  • 二將俱不得其中 - Means 'both generals fail to strike a balance.'
  • 不得其死 - Means 'do not die in a fitting manner' or 'do not achieve a proper death.'
  • 雄豪之中 - Means 'among the powerful and heroic.'
  • 賢達之風 - Means 'the spirit of the wise and virtuous.'
  • 天生蒸人 - Means 'Heaven has created all people.'
  • 必有司牧 - Means 'there must be a leader to guide them.'
  • 當今為牧 - Means 'the current leader.'
  • 非子而誰 - Means 'if not you, then who?'
  • 老夫年餘知命 - Means 'an old man who has passed the age of knowing his fate.'
  • 願不及此 - Means 'I no longer have such ambitions.'
  • 欣戴大弟 - Means 'joyfully support the elder brother.'
  • 攀鱗附翼 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'to attach oneself to a powerful person.'
  • 唯冀早膺圖箓 - Means 'only hoping to早日接受天命' (to soon accept the mandate of heaven).
  • 以寧兆庶 - Means 'to bring peace to the people.'
  • 宗盟之長 - Means 'the leader of the alliance.'
  • 屬籍見容 - Means 'be accepted as part of the clan.'
  • 復封於唐 - Means 'to be enfeoffed in Tang.'
  • 斯榮足矣 - Means 'this honor is enough.'
  • 殪商辛於牧野 - Means 'to defeat Shang Xin at Muye.'
  • 所不忍言 - Means 'it is不忍言' (it is不忍言) (it is不忍言).
  • 執子嬰於咸阳 - Means 'to capture Zi Ying in Xianyang.'
  • 非敢聞命 - Means 'not敢听命' (not敢听命) (not敢听命).
  • 密得書甚悅 - Means 'Li Mi was very pleased after receiving the letter.'
  • 示其部下 - Means 'showed it to his subordinates.'
  • 唐公見推 - Means 'Tang Gong is recognized as the leader.'
  • 天下不足可定 - Means 'the world can be easily pacified.'
  • 後密兵敗 - Means 'later, Li Mi's army was defeated.'
  • 王伯當保河陽 - Means 'Wang Berdang defended Heyang.'
  • 密以輕騎歸之 - Means 'Li Mi returned with a small group of cavalry.'
  • 請以謝眾 - Means 'to apologize to the troops.'
  • 諸公幸不相棄 - Means 'please do not abandon me.'
  • 當共歸關中 - Means 'let us all return to Guanzhong together.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 關侯 - Refers to Guan Yu (關羽), a famous general from the Three Kingdoms period, often referred to as 'Lord Guan' or 'Lord Guan of the Gate.'
  • 蜀將 - Refers to a general from the state of Shu during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 簡文 - Refers to Emperor Simian (簡文帝), a ruler of the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 道光武 - A respectful way of referring to Emperor Guangwu (光武帝), the founder of the Eastern Han dynasty.
  • 漢世祖 - Refers to Emperor Guangwu (光武帝), the founder of the Eastern Han dynasty.
  • 魏武 - Refers to Cao Cao (曹操), a prominent warlord and statesman of the late Eastern Han dynasty.
  • 報李密書 - Means 'reply letter to Li Mi.'
  • 【出獨異志】 - A reference to a specific text or historical record, indicating the source of the information.
  • 【出簡文談疏】 - A reference to a specific text or historical record, indicating the source of the information.

Proper Nouns

  • 郭齊宗 - A person's name, likely referring to a historical figure or official.
  • 唐休璟 - Another person's name, likely a historical figure.
  • 李盡忠 - A person's name, likely a historical figure.
  • 封常清 - A person's name, likely a historical figure.
  • 李光弼 - A person's name, likely a historical figure.
  • 李密 - A historical figure, likely referring to Li Mi.
  • 王伯當 - A historical figure, likely referring to Wang Berdang.
  • 商辛 - A historical figure, likely referring to Shang Xin.
  • 子嬰 - A historical figure, likely referring to Zi Ying.

[2231]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 及至京 - Means 'upon reaching the capital,' referring to the protagonist arriving in the capital city.
  • 礼数益薄 - Means 'the etiquette became increasingly sparse,' indicating a decline in the level of respect or courtesy shown.
  • 执政者又来求财 - Means 'the officials came to seek wealth,' suggesting that the officials were after material gain.
  • 意甚不平 - Means 'he was very displeased,' expressing a strong negative emotional reaction.
  • 寻拜光禄卿 - Means 'soon was appointed as the Minister of the Imperial Clan,' indicating a promotion in official rank.
  • 封邢国公 - Means 'bestowed the title of Duke of Xing,' indicating a noble title.
  • 未几闻其所部将帅皆不附世充 - Means 'shortly after, he heard that his subordinates and generals were not loyal to Shi Chong,' indicating a loss of loyalty among his troops.
  • 故时者以经略王充王伯当为左武卫 - Means 'at that time, Wang Chong and Wang Badang were appointed as Left Guard General,' indicating their military roles.
  • 亦令副密行至桃林 - Means 'also ordered them to accompany Mi to Taolin,' indicating a secondary role in the mission.
  • 密惧谋叛 - Means 'Mi feared and plotted rebellion,' indicating a plan to rebel.
  • 伯当止密不从 - Means 'Wang Badang tried to stop Mi but he did not listen,' indicating a failed attempt to prevent rebellion.
  • 密据桃林县城驱掠畜产 - Means 'Mi seized control of Taolin County and drove off livestock,' indicating a violent act.
  • 横出撃之遂斩密 - Means 'he emerged and attacked, ultimately killing Mi,' indicating a decisive military action.
  • 年三十七 - Means 'he was thirty-seven years old,' providing the age at the time of death.
  • 时徐勣在阳为密坚守 - Means 'at that time, Xu Jue was in Xiangyang, defending for Mi,' indicating Xu Jue's loyalty to Mi.
  • 勣发丧行服备君臣之礼 - Means 'Xu Jue mourned and prepared the rites of a subject to his lord,' indicating a formal funeral ritual.
  • 表请收塟 - Means 'submitted a memorial requesting burial,' indicating a formal request.
  • 大具威仪 - Means 'with great ceremony,' indicating a grand funeral.
  • 三军皆缟素 - Means 'all the troops wore white,' indicating mourning attire.
  • 塟于阳山南五里 - Means 'buried in the south of Xiangyang Mountain, five li away,' indicating the burial location.
  • 故人哭之多有呕血者 - Means 'old friends wept so much that many vomited blood,' indicating intense grief.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 密 - Refers to Li Mi, a prominent general during the early Tang Dynasty.
  • 世充 - Refers to Shi Chong, a rival warlord during the late Sui Dynasty.
  • 光禄卿 - A high-ranking official in the imperial court, responsible for the imperial clan.
  • 邢国公 - A noble title bestowed upon Li Mi.
  • 黎阳 - A place name, likely referring to a strategic location during the conflict.
  • 左武卫 - A military title, indicating a high-ranking general.
  • 桃林 - A place name, likely referring to a strategic location during the conflict.
  • 徐勣 - A loyal general who defended for Li Mi.
  • 阳 - A place name, likely referring to a strategic location during the conflict.

Proper Nouns

  • 密 - Li Mi.
  • 世充 - Shi Chong.
  • 光禄卿 - A high-ranking official in the imperial court.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 討史思明師 - To campaign against the army of Shi Siming. The verb '討' means to campaign or attack, and '師' refers to an army or military force.
  • 既夕還軍 - After dusk, he withdrew his army. A compact way of saying that after the night fell, the army retreated.
  • 留其卒一千人 - Left behind one thousand of his soldiers. The structure is elliptical but clear.
  • 謂雍颢曰 - He said to Yong Hao. A standard formula for reporting dialogue.
  • 万人敌 - An idiom meaning 'a warrior who can fight ten thousand men,' i.e., a formidable general.
  • 劫 - To attack or ambush.
  • 我且去之 - I will leave them behind. The phrase is elliptical but clear in context.
  • 子领卒待贼于此 - You lead the soldiers and wait for the enemy here. This is a command to Yong Hao.
  • 至勿与战 - When they come, do not fight them. A direct command.
  • 降则俱来 - If they surrender, they will all come. The verb '俱来' means 'all come together.'
  • 命曰必获李君不然无归 - He ordered, 'You must capture Li Jun. If you do not, you will not return.' This is a direct quote with a conditional threat.
  • 压 - To press against or attack.
  • 颢阻濠休卒吟啸相视 - Yong Hao blocked the moat and his soldiers rested, singing and shouting at each other. This is a vivid description of the soldiers’ idle state.
  • 怪之 - Found this strange. The verb '怪' here is used in the sense of 'to find strange' or 'to be puzzled by.'
  • 太尉 - A high-ranking military title, often used for a general or commander-in-chief.
  • 兵几何曰千人 - How many soldiers do you have? A thousand. This is a question and answer pair.
  • 将谓谁曰雍颢也 - Who is the general? It is Yong Hao. This is a dialogue.
  • 沉吟久谓其下曰 - He pondered for a long time and said to his subordinates. The verb '沉吟' means to think deeply or hesitate.
  • 我受命必得李君今获颢不塞此望必见害不如降之 - I was ordered to capture Li Jun. Now that I have captured Yong Hao, this will not satisfy my commander, and I will be punished. It would be better to surrender. This is a complex sentence with internal logic.
  • 遂请降颢与之俱至光弼 - So he requested surrender, and Yong Hao went with him to Li Guangbi. This is the conclusion of the event.
  • 又尝伏军守河阳与史思明相持经年 - He also once stationed troops at Heyang to hold off Shi Siming for a full year. This is a historical account of a military standoff.
  • 洗马于河南以示其多 - He washed his warhorses in the south of the river to show how many he had. This is a symbolic gesture to demonstrate strength.
  • 牝马 - Female horses. Used in the context of a military deception tactic.
  • 尽驱出 - He drove them all out. This is a military maneuver to mislead the enemy.

  • 史思明 - Shi Siming (703–761), a rebel general who later declared himself emperor during the An Lushan Rebellion. He was a major adversary of Li Guangbi.
  • 雍颢 - Yong Hao, a subordinate or military officer under Li Guangbi.
  • 太尉 - A high-ranking military title, often used for a general or commander-in-chief.
  • 河阳 - Heyang, a strategic location in northern China, a key site in the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 洗马 - Washing horses, a practice to maintain and show off warhorses, often used symbolically to demonstrate military strength.
  • 牝马 - Female horses, used in the context of a military deception tactic.
  • 经年 - A full year, indicating the prolonged nature of the military standoff.

  • 更茶数瓯 - Means 'to drink several cups of tea again.'
  • 张灯 - Means 'to light the lamps' or 'to light the candles.'
  • 不交一言 - Means 'not exchanging a single word.'
  • 口中咀少物 - Means 'chewing a small amount of something in his mouth.'
  • 遥观一如交谈之状 - Means 'looking as if they were talking from a distance.'
  • 动有警察 - Means 'always being on guard' or 'always being vigilant.'
  • 密报之 - Means 'to secretly report to him.'
  • 相国与尔自旦至暮所话何 - Means 'what did the chancellor and you talk about from morning until night?'
  • 殊不信 - Means 'not believing at all' or 'not trusting at all.'
  • 谓其不诚 - Means 'thinking him dishonest' or 'considering him untrustworthy.'
  • 戮之 - Means 'to kill him' or 'to execute him.'
  • 假途归京 - Means 'to take a detour back to the capital.'
  • 不令暂起 - Means 'not allowing him to leave for a moment.'
  • 留晩食 - Means 'to keep him for a late meal.'
  • 食讫已晡时 - Means 'after the meal, it was already late afternoon.'
  • 又不令起 - Means 'not allowing him to leave again.'
  • 即更茶数瓯 - Means 'then drinking several more cups of tea.'
  • 至张灯乃许辞去 - Means 'until the lamps were lit, he was allowed to leave.'
  • 自旦及暮 - Means 'from morning until night.'

  • 持两端而求利也 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'to maintain a neutral stance while seeking profit.'
  • 潜禀于都押衙 - Means 'secretly consult with the city commander (都押衙).'
  • 俟可否 - Means 'await approval or disapproval.'
  • 乖方 - Means 'to go wrong' or 'to deviate.'
  • 教甚纷纭 - Means 'the instructions became very complicated.'
  • 时帅臣多是文儒 - Means 'at that time, the military leaders were mostly scholars and literati.'
  • 不欲生事 - Means 'did not want to create trouble.'
  • 姑息蛮蜑 - Means 'to indulge or tolerate the barbarians.'
  • 凭凌 - Means 'to rely on or depend on.'
  • 若无亭障 - Means 'as if there were no defensive structures.'

  • 十年不敢犯境 - Means 'for ten years, they dared not invade the border.'
  • 末年命大将许存征蛮 - Means 'in his later years, he ordered General Xu Cun to campaign against the barbarians.'
  • 泄漏军机 - Means 'leaked military secrets.'
  • 召三王而斩之 - Means 'summoned the three kings and executed them.'

  • 时不利也 - Means 'it was not the right time.'
  • 有非常之功许公之谓也 - Means 'this is what is meant by having extraordinary merit, referring to General Xu Cun.'

  • 朝廷次第文字 - Means 'the court arranged the documents.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 恣其劫掠以自給 - Allows them to plunder for their own sustenance, describing a state of lawlessness.
  • 然后烹豕犬遣其妇女羞馔恣其饮噉 - Then they cooked pigs and dogs and sent the women to prepare food, allowing them to eat freely, describing a cruel act of feasting.
  • 其妇女多取之熬捣一如竦末置于食味中 - The women took them, ground them up, and mixed them into the food, describing the preparation of a poisoned meal.
  • 然后饮以浊醪 - Then they gave them a drink of turbid liquor, indicating the administration of the poison.
  • 于时药作竟于腰下防劔掘地曰马入地下去也 - At that time, the poison took effect, and they drew their swords from their waists, digging the ground and saying, 'The horse has gone into the ground,' describing the victims' final actions.
  • 或欲入火投渊顚而后仆 - Some wanted to jump into the fire, others into the water, and they fell down, indicating the victims' desperate reactions.
  • 其马使人逐官路棰而遣之罔有知者 - Their horses were sent down the official road, beaten and driven away, with no one knowing, describing the disposal of the horses.
  • 后地土改易方泄其事 - Later, when the land was changed, the matter was revealed, indicating the eventual uncovering of the crime.

[2251]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 仕郡功曹 - A classical term referring to a position in the local government. '仕' means to serve or hold office; '郡' is a prefecture or county; '功曹' is a local official, often the chief administrative officer of a county.
  • 僣号 - A term used to describe someone who usurps the title of an emperor or ruler, i.e., 'to usurp a title' or 'to claim imperial status improperly.'
  • 拥郡人不伏述 - '拥' means to support or protect; '郡人' is the people of the prefecture; '不伏述' is a variant of '不从述' or '不从述', meaning 'did not follow' or 'did not submit to' the usurper.
  • 埋车绊马而战死 - A vivid expression of dying in battle, with '埋车绊马' meaning to bury one's chariot and tie up one's horse, implying a desperate stand or final battle.
  • 追赠辅汉将军 - '追赠' means to posthumously bestow a title; '辅汉将军' is a military title given to someone who aided the Han dynasty.
  • 表为置祠 - A phrase meaning 'to recommend [the establishment] of a shrine' or 'to request the establishment of a temple.'
  • 失首退至此地 - '失首' literally means 'lost head,' but in this context likely refers to having one's head severed or being decapitated; '退至此地' means 'retreated to this place.'
  • 绊马讫以手摸头始知失首 - A sequence of events: '绊马' (tied up the horse), '讫' (afterward), '以手摸头' (touched one’s head), and '始知失首' (then realized one had lost one’s head). This is a vivid but somewhat macabre description of dying in battle and only realizing one’s decapitation after the fact.
  • 土人感而义之 - '土人' refers to the local people or residents of the area; '感' means to feel moved or inspired; '义之' means to regard it as righteous or virtuous.
  • 号为健儿庙 - A name given to a temple; '健儿' could be translated as 'brave soldier' or 'heroic youth.'
  • 身长八尺 - A classical way of describing a person’s height. In the Han dynasty, one '尺' (chi) was about 23–24 cm, so 8 chi would be about 1.84–1.92 meters.
  • 姿容雄伟 - A phrase describing a person’s appearance as imposing or majestic.
  • 偃旗皷 - '偃旗' means to lower the flag; '皷' is a variant of '鼓' (drum), so '偃旗皷' is likely a corruption or miswriting of '偃旗息鼓,' a classical idiom meaning to lower the flag and silence the drums, indicating a retreat or cessation of battle.
  • 射之公军大骇死者甚多 - '射之' means to shoot at them; '公军' refers to the army of Cao Cao (the 'public' or 'imperial' army); '大骇' means greatly frightened; '死者甚多' means many were killed.
  • 子龙一身都是胆也 - A direct quote from Liu Bei, praising Zhao Yun for his courage and bravery. '一身都是胆' is an idiomatic expression meaning 'his whole body is full of courage' or 'he is all bravery.'
  • 呵叱而前 - A phrase meaning to shout and command while advancing.
  • 贫贱难可居 - '贫贱' means poverty and low status; '难可居' means difficult to endure or hard to live with.
  • 设有功富贵可致 - '设' is a conditional particle meaning 'if'; '有功' means to have merit or achieve success; '富贵可致' means wealth and nobility can be attained.
  • 不探虎穴安得虎子 - A well-known idiom meaning 'without entering the tiger’s den, how can one catch the tiger’s cub?' It is used to justify taking risks for great rewards.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 朱遵 - A historical figure from the Eastern Han dynasty who resisted the usurper Gengshi (公孫述) and died in battle. He was posthumously honored by Emperor Guangwu (光武).
  • 公孫述 - A warlord who declared himself emperor during the fall of the Western Han and established the short-lived Cheng Han kingdom in Sichuan. He was eventually defeated by the Eastern Han under Emperor Guangwu.
  • 光武 - Emperor Guangwu of Han (r. 25–57 CE), the founder of the Eastern Han dynasty. He is known for reunifying China after the fall of the Western Han.
  • 吴汉 - A general under Emperor Guangwu. He is known for his loyalty and military prowess.
  • 赵云 - A famous general of the Shu Han state during the Three Kingdoms period, known for his loyalty and bravery. He is one of the most celebrated generals in Chinese history.
  • 刘备 - The founder of the Shu Han state during the Three Kingdoms period, and a key figure in the *Romance of the Three Kingdoms*.
  • 曹公 - A respectful and honorific term for Cao Cao, the warlord and de facto ruler of the north during the late Eastern Han and Three Kingdoms period.
  • 吕防 - A general from the state of Wu during the Three Kingdoms period. His story is recounted in the *Duyi Zhi* (独异志), a collection of unusual or extraordinary tales.
  • 邓当 - The husband of Lu Su’s sister, and the father of Lu Meng, who was a general in the state of Wu.
  • 独异志 - A classical text compiling unusual or extraordinary stories, often with a moral or didactic purpose.
  • 新津县图经 - A local gazetteer or geographical record of Xinjin County, likely from the Tang or Song dynasty, which includes historical anecdotes and local lore.
  • 赵云别传 - A separate biography or account of Zhao Yun, possibly from the *Sanguozhi Yizhi* (三国志注) or other later sources.

Proper Nouns

  • 朱遵 - Zhu Zun
  • 公孫述 - Gengshi (Gongsun Shu)
  • 光武 - Emperor Guangwu of Han
  • 吴汉 - Wu Han
  • 赵云 - Zhao Yun (Zilong)
  • 刘备 - Liu Bei
  • 曹公 - Cao Cao
  • 吕防 - Lu Fang

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 魏任城王彰 - Refers to Cao Zhi, a prince of the state of Wei during the Three Kingdoms period, known for his literary and military achievements.
  • 武帝 - Refers to Emperor Wu of Wei, Cao Zhi's father, a significant figure in the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 六韬洪范之书 - Refers to the 'Six Secret Teachings' (六韬) and the 'Great Plan' (洪范), important texts in Chinese military and political thought.
  • 诵六韬洪范之书数千言 - Reciting thousands of words from these books, indicating a deep and extensive study.
  • 武帝谋伐吴问彰取其利师之决 - Emperor Wu consulted Cao Zhi about the advantages and strategies for attacking Wu, another state during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 百步中于悬发 - Accurately hitting a target from a hundred steps away, a demonstration of exceptional archery skill.
  • 乐浪国献彪虎文如锦斑 - The kingdom of Lelang (present-day North Korea) presented a tiger with a coat like brocade, symbolizing its ferocity and beauty.
  • 以铁为槛骁勇之徒莫敢轻视 - The tiger was kept in an iron cage, and no brave warriors dared to underestimate it.
  • 曳虎尾以绕臂 - Pulling the tiger's tail around his arm, a demonstration of extraordinary bravery and control.
  • 虎弭耳无声 - The tiger lay down quietly, showing submission and respect.
  • 吞并吴蜀如鸱衔腐防耳 - Conquering Wu and Shu would be as easy as a kite carrying away a piece of rotten meat, a metaphor for ease and dominance.
  • 如汉东平王塟礼 - Buried according to the funeral rites of the Han Dynasty's Prince of Dongping, indicating a high level of respect and honor.
  • 四方闻其神勇皆寝兵自固 - Hearing of his divine courage, people in all directions laid down their arms and fortified themselves, showing the impact of his reputation.
  • 桓石防 - Refers to Huan Shihfang, a military general of the Eastern Jin Dynasty known for his bravery and skill.
  • 有材干趫捷绝伦 - Possessing exceptional talent and agility, surpassing all others.
  • 随父豁在荆州 - Accompanied his father, Huan Hui, in Jingzhou, a region in central China, highlighting his family's influence and his own capabilities.
  • 见猛兽被数箭而伏 - Saw a fierce beast that was wounded by several arrows and lying down, a test of courage.
  • 因急往防一箭 - Quickly went to shoot an arrow, demonstrating his quick reflexes and skill.
  • 猛虎踞跃 - The tiger jumped up, showing its strength and ferocity.
  • 复防一箭而归 - Shot another arrow and returned, completing the challenge.
  • 手顿其鼻象伏不动 - Touched the elephant's trunk, causing it to lie down without moving, a demonstration of control and authority.
  • 铸万钧钟置崇华殿前 - Cast a massive bell weighing thousands of catties and placed it in front of the Chonghua Hall, a symbol of power and grandeur.
  • 欲徙之力士百人引之不动 - Tried to move the bell, but even a hundred strong men could not budge it, emphasizing its weight and the difficulty of moving it.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 魏任城王彰 - Cao Zhi, a prince of the state of Wei, known for his literary and military achievements.
  • 武帝 - Emperor Wu of Wei, Cao Zhi's father, a significant figure in the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 六韬洪范之书 - Important texts in Chinese military and political thought, emphasizing strategic and philosophical knowledge.
  • 乐浪国 - The kingdom of Lelang, located in present-day North Korea, known for its contributions to the region.
  • 汉东平王塟礼 - The funeral rites of the Han Dynasty's Prince of Dongping, indicating a high level of respect and honor.
  • 桓石防 - Huan Shihfang, a military general of the Eastern Jin Dynasty, known for his bravery and skill.
  • 随父豁在荆州 - Accompanied his father, Huan Hui, in Jingzhou, a region in central China, highlighting his family's influence and his own capabilities.
  • 崇华殿 - The Chonghua Hall, a significant building in the imperial palace, symbolizing power and grandeur.
  • 东晋初藏于秘阁 - The records were kept in the secret library during the early Eastern Jin Dynasty, indicating the importance and confidentiality of the information.

[2253]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 少有胆气 - Means 'had courage and boldness from a young age.'
  • 跳走如飞 - Describes someone who could run or move as fast as a bird, indicating agility and speed.
  • 为尚书出一技 - Literally means 'to perform a skill for the Secretary (尚书),' but in context, it refers to demonstrating a particular ability.
  • 瞠眸奋发 - Means 'to gaze fiercely and strive with energy,' indicating intense effort or determination.
  • 代推其骁果 - Means 'generations have praised his bravery and decisiveness,' emphasizing his reputation for valor.
  • 有勇力 - Means 'having strength and courage,' describing physical prowess.
  • 日行五百里 - Means 'able to travel five hundred li in a day,' emphasizing extraordinary speed.

  • 不虞用诚之子居后引刀斫勋 - '不虞' means 'did not expect,' '居后' means 'behind,' and '斫' means 'to strike with a sword.' The phrase indicates an unexpected attack from behind.

  • 胸前小镜子 - Refers to a small mirror on the chest.
  • 十五夜月圆如镜 - Means 'the moon is full and round like a mirror on the fifteenth night.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 蓬山 - Refers to Penglai Mountain, a mythical mountain in Chinese mythology associated with immortals.
  • 明珰玉女 - Refers to a jade maiden, a common motif in classical Chinese poetry and literature.
  • 深宫 - Refers to the inner palace, often associated with the emperor's harem.
  • 昆仑奴 - Refers to a slave from the Kunlun Mountains, often depicted in classical Chinese literature as a loyal and powerful servant.

  • 其猛如虎 - Its ferocity is like that of a tiger.
  • 即曹州孟海之犬也 - It is the dog from Caozhou, Menghai.
  • 世间非老奴不能毙此犬 - In the world, only an old servant can kill this dog.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 曹州孟海 - A specific location in China, possibly a town or village known for its dogs.
  • 歌妓院 - A courtesan's courtyard, a place where courtesans lived and entertained guests.

[2285]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 此生具告磨勒之谋 - This life has fully informed Mo Le of the plan; '具告' means 'fully informed' and '谋' means 'plan.'
  • 荷而至姬曰 - Carrying it to the lady, she said; '荷' means 'to carry' and '至姬' means 'to the lady.'
  • 某家本富居在朔方 - My family was originally wealthy and lived in the northern frontier; '本富' means 'originally wealthy' and '朔方' refers to the northern frontier.
  • 尚且偷生 - Still managing to survive; '尚且' means 'still' and '偷生' means 'to manage to survive.'
  • 脸虽铅华心颇郁结 - Her face may be adorned with makeup, but her heart is deeply troubled; '脸虽' means 'her face may be,' '铅华' refers to makeup, and '心颇郁结' means 'her heart is deeply troubled.'
  • 尚且偷生脸虽铅华心颇郁结 - Combines the ideas of surviving despite hardship and the contrast between outward appearance and inner turmoil.
  • 纵玉筯举馔金鑪泛香云屏而每进绮罗綉被而常眠珠翠皆非所愿如在桎梏 - Even if she serves jade chopsticks and exquisite meals, and sleeps on embroidered silk and adorned with jewels, she does not wish for it, as if in shackles; '纵' means 'even if,' '玉筯' refers to jade chopsticks, '举馔' means 'to serve meals,' '金鑪泛香' means 'gold furnace emits fragrance,' '云屏' refers to cloud-like screens, '绮罗綉被' means 'embroidered silk and quilts,' '珠翠' refers to jewels, and '桎梏' means 'shackles.'
  • 贤爪牙既有神术何妨为脱狴牢 - Since you, the wise and powerful, have divine skills, why not help her escape from the prison; '贤爪牙' means 'wise and powerful,' '神术' means 'divine skills,' and '脱狴牢' means 'escape from prison.'
  • 所愿既申虽死不悔请为仆隷愿侍光容 - Once her wishes are fulfilled, she will not regret even death and will serve as a servant, willing to attend to your presence; '所愿既申' means 'once her wishes are fulfilled,' '虽死不悔' means 'even if she dies, she will not regret,' '请为仆隷' means 'will serve as a servant,' and '愿侍光容' means 'willing to attend to your presence.'
  • 又不知郎君髙意如何 - I do not know what high intention you have; '郎君' means 'young master' or 'lord,' and '髙意' means 'high intention.'
  • 生愀然不语 - He looked solemn and did not speak; '愀然' means 'solemnly' and '不语' means 'did not speak.'
  • 娘子既坚确如是此亦小事耳 - Since you are so determined, this is also a minor matter; '娘子' means 'lady,' '坚确' means 'determined,' and '此亦小事耳' means 'this is also a minor matter.'
  • 恐迟明遂生与姬而飞出峻垣十余重一品家之守御无有警者遂归学院而匿之 - Fearing daylight, he then took the man and the lady and flew out through more than ten layers of high walls; the defense of the noble family's residence was not alarmed, and they returned to the academy and hid there; '恐迟明' means 'fearing daylight,' '飞出峻垣十余重' means 'flew out through more than ten layers of high walls,' '一品家之守御无有警者' means 'the defense of the noble family's residence was not alarmed,' and '遂归学院而匿之' means 'they returned to the academy and hid there.'
  • 及旦一品家方觉又见犬已毙一品大骇曰 - By dawn, the noble family realized it, and saw the dog had died; the noble was greatly startled and said; '及旦' means 'by dawn,' '一品家方觉' means 'the noble family realized it,' '又见犬已毙' means 'saw the dog had died,' and '一品大骇曰' means 'the noble was greatly startled and said.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 列火炬出迎僧延韦生坐一防中 - Lit torches to welcome a monk who then invited Wei Sheng to sit in a protective enclosure.
  • 郎君勿忧 - Polite expression meaning 'Do not worry, sir.'
  • 夫人下处如法无复 - Likely means 'Your wife's quarters are arranged according to proper etiquette and there is nothing to worry about.'
  • 郎君且自慰安之 - Means 'Sir, please comfort yourself.'
  • 适来贫道所中郎君弹悉在乃举手搦脑后五丸坠焉 - Just now, all your arrows were in the place, then he raised his hand and plucked five pellets from the back of his head, letting them fall.
  • 有顷布筵具蒸犊犊上劄刀子十余以韲饼环之 - In a short while, he set out a table with a steamed calf, with more than ten small knives on the calf, surrounded by steamed cakes.
  • 贫道有义弟数人欲令谒见 - I have several brothers who I wish to introduce to you.
  • 言已朱衣巨者五六辈列于阶下 - After saying this, five or six people in red clothes with large girdles lined up at the bottom of the stairs.
  • 拜郎君汝等向遇郎君即成韲粉矣 - Bow to Sir, if you had met Sir before, you would have become steamed flour.
  • 食毕僧曰贫道久为此业今向迟暮欲改前非不幸有一子技过老僧欲 - After eating, the monk said, 'I have been doing this for a long time, now I am old, I want to change my past mistakes, unfortunately, I have a son whose skill surpasses mine, I want to...'

  • 贫道 - A humble self-reference for a monk.
  • 义弟 - Brothers who are not blood-related but are considered as close as family.
  • 朱衣巨者 - People in red clothes with large girdles, possibly indicating a specific social or religious status.
  • 蒸犊 - Steamed calf, a traditional dish in Chinese cuisine.
  • 韲饼 - Steamed cakes, another traditional dish.

  • 飛飛當堂執一短鞭 - The young woman holds a short whip in the middle of the hall.

[2290]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 應進士舉 - To take the imperial examination for the *jinshi* degree, a key step in the classical Chinese civil service examination system.
  • 京中無第宅 - In the capital, there was no residence; meaning the person had no property or house in the capital.
  • 常赁人隙院居止 - Often rented a vacant courtyard from others to live in; referring to temporary or shared housing.
  • 主人别在一院 - The owner lived in a different courtyard; indicating that the owner and the tenant were in separate parts of the compound.
  • 都无丈夫 - There was no man at all; suggesting that the owner was absent or the household was run by a woman.
  • 窥之亦有容色 - She looked at him and had a beautiful appearance; indicating that the woman was attractive and noticed him.
  • 遣通意求纳为妻 - Sent someone to convey the intention of marrying him as a wife; showing a formal proposal.
  • 求以为妾许之 - She asked to be his concubine and agreed to it; indicating a lower-status marriage.
  • 不肯言其姓 - Would not say her surname; suggesting she was hiding her identity or of noble lineage.
  • 崔所取给妇人无倦色 - The woman never showed fatigue in providing for him; indicating her dedication.
  • 产一子数月矣 - Gave birth to a child several months ago; indicating the child is now a few months old.
  • 夜崔寝及闭戸垂帷而已 - At night, when Cui slept, he closed the door and hung the curtain; suggesting he was cautious or suspicious.
  • 忽失其妇 - Suddenly, he lost his wife; indicating she disappeared.
  • 意其有奸 - Suspected her of infidelity; showing his suspicion.
  • 发忿怒 - He became angry and frustrated; showing his emotional reaction.
  • 徬徨而行 - Wandered around in the courtyard; indicating his restlessness.
  • 月胧明 - The moon was dimly bright; setting a somber, mysterious atmosphere.
  • 自屋而下以白练纒身 - Came down from the house wrapped in a white silk cloth; indicating a ghostly or supernatural appearance.
  • 右手持首 - Right hand held a head; referring to a decapitated head (the character is likely a variant or damaged character, possibly *刀* or *剑*, but here it is likely *首* for 'head').
  • 左手防一人头 - Left hand held another person's head; indicating she carried two heads.
  • 言其父昔枉为郡守所杀 - She said her father was wrongfully killed by a county magistrate; indicating a past injustice.
  • 入城求报已数年矣 - She had entered the city to seek revenge for several years; indicating a long-standing vendetta.
  • 今既尅矣不可久留 - Now that I have succeeded, I cannot stay long; indicating she has completed her mission.
  • 请从此辞 - Please let me take my leave; a formal farewell.
  • 更结束其身 - She re-dressed herself; indicating she was preparing to leave.
  • 以灰囊盛人首防之 - She placed the heads in a sack of ash and carried them; indicating a macabre action.
  • 某幸得为君妾二年而已 - I have been your concubine for only two years; indicating the short duration of their relationship.
  • 有一子宅及二婢皆自致并以奉赠 - A child, a house, and two maids—all I have achieved, I give to you; indicating she is leaving her possessions behind.
  • 养育孩子 - To raise the child; indicating her final act of care.
  • 言讫而别 - After speaking, she departed; marking the end of the interaction.
  • 逾墙越舍而去 - She leapt over walls and left the house; showing her supernatural ability to escape.
  • 惊叹未已少顷却至 - He was still amazed, but soon she returned; indicating her return after leaving.
  • 适去忘哺孩子少乳 - I just left and forgot to feed the child a little milk; indicating her last act of care before leaving.
  • 餧儿已毕便永去矣 - After feeding the child, I will go away forever; indicating her final farewell.
  • 久之怪不闻婴儿啼 - After a long time, he was surprised that the baby did not cry; indicating the child's death.
  • 杀其子者以絶其念也 - She killed the child to cut off his thoughts; indicating her final act to end his attachment.
  • 古之侠莫能过焉 - Among ancient knights, none surpassed her; indicating her heroic and tragic nature.
  • 出原化记 - From *Yuanhua Ji*; the source of the story.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 崔慎思 - Cui Shensi, a name of a historical or fictional figure in the story.
  • 唐贞元中 - During the *Zhenyuan* period of the Tang Dynasty (785–805 CE); indicating the historical setting.
  • 进士举 - The *jinshi* examination; one of the highest levels of the imperial examination system.
  • 赁人隙院 - Renting a vacant courtyard; a common practice for those without property in the capital.
  • 郡守 - A county magistrate; a local official in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 侠 - 'Knight-errant' or 'chivalrous hero'; a common trope in Chinese folklore and literature.
  • 原化记 - *Yuanhua Ji* (《原化记》), a classical Chinese collection of ghost stories and supernatural tales, likely from the Tang or Song Dynasty.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • Careful parsing of long sentences like '三光人皆长三丈被羽衣奏竒乐非世所闻也' to ensure clarity and coherence.
  • Reading sequences of actions and requests as a single, coherent narrative, such as '便告请求哀长人语令前去从命进道'.
  • Breaking down detailed descriptions into manageable parts, such as '凡遇如此者九处最后所至苦告饥馁'.
  • Ensuring logical progression in sequences of actions, such as '长人取之次捋亦取后捋令啗食即得疗饥'.
  • Managing complex sentences by breaking them into parts, such as '请问九处之名求停不去答曰君命不得停还问张华当悉此人便复随穴而行遂得出交郡徃还六七年间即归洛问华以所得二物视之华云如尘者是黄河龙涎泥是昆山下泥九处地仙名九馆羊爲痴龙其初一珠食之与天地等夀次者延年后者充饥而已'.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '博闻多奇' can be read as a compound adjective describing Sheng's extensive knowledge and unique insights.
  • The phrase '旁稽异术' can be read as a verb phrase, indicating the act of investigating various different methods or techniques.
  • The phrase '博学求之' can be read as a verb phrase, indicating the act of seeking knowledge broadly from many sources.
  • The phrase '无不利于事也' can be read as a concluding statement, emphasizing the practical benefits of learning from all social strata.
  • The sentence '灵帝时，洛阳女子生时两头四臂' can be read as a compact description of a woman giving birth to a child with a rare physical condition.
  • The sentence '灵帝时，洛阳有女子，生时两头四臂' can be read as a more complete version, making the implied subject explicit.
  • The phrase '生时两头四臂' can be read as modifying the woman, indicating that at the time of her birth she had two heads and four arms, but '女子生时两头四臂' can also read as 'when she gave birth, [the baby] had two heads and four arms.'
  • The simile '凡为文章，犹乘骐骥' can be read as introducing a broad statement about literary composition.
  • The concessive pattern '虽有逸气，当以衔勒制之' can be read as acknowledging natural brilliance but emphasizing the need for restraint.
  • The final prohibition '勿使流乱轨躅，放意填坑岸也' can be read as two linked dangers: straying from the proper track and letting the writing run off into structural disorder.
  • The opening rhetorical comparison '自古明王圣帝，犹须勤学，况凡庶乎' can be read as arguing from the greater case to the lesser one.
  • The clause '此事遍于经史' can be read as elliptical but meaning that examples of this principle are found throughout the classics and histories.
  • The closing sequence '吾亦不能郑重，聊举近世切要，以启寤汝耳' can be read as compressing concession, limitation, and purpose into a single compact statement.
  • The formal thesis statement '夫文章者，原出五经' can be read as introducing a broad statement about the origins of literary writing.
  • The long parallel sequence '诏命策檄，生于书者也；序述论议，生于易者也；歌咏赋颂，生于诗者也；祭祀哀诔，生于礼者也；书奏箴铭，生于春秋者也' can be read as a five-part mapping between genres and classics.
  • The sentence '朝廷宪章，军旅誓诰，敷显仁义，发明功德，牧民建国，不可暂无' can be read as shifting from taxonomy to function, arguing that writing is indispensable in government and public life.
  • The clause '至于陶冶性灵，从容讽谏，入其滋味，亦乐事也' can be read as turning from public utility to moral cultivation and literary pleasure.
  • The final conditional '行有余力，则可习之' can be read as narrowing the recommendation and giving the passage a measured close.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 临薨却进门人作表皆不如意 - This phrase is a bit ambiguous. It could mean 'when on the verge of death, his disciples wrote a memorial (表) but none satisfied him' or 'when on the verge of death, his disciples tried to write a memorial (表) but none were satisfactory.'
  • 既不敢将归地下，又不合留在人间 - This phrase can be read as two linked clauses: 'it is neither appropriate to take it to the netherworld nor to leave it in the world.'
  • 闻者叹其简切而不乱 - This phrase can be read as a single clause: 'those who heard were amazed by its simplicity and clarity without confusion.'
  • 樊素善歌，小蛮善舞 - This phrase can be read as two linked clauses: 'Fan Su was good at singing, and Xiao Man was good at dancing.'
  • 年既高迈而小蛮方丰艳 - This phrase can be read as two linked clauses: 'when he was old and advanced in age, Xiao Man was still full of beauty and charm.'
  • 因杨柳词以托意 - This phrase can be read as a single clause: 'because of the willow tree poem, he used it to express his feelings.'
  • 永丰在何处 - This phrase can be read as a single clause: 'where is the Yongfeng Pavilion?'
  • 遂因东使命取永丰柳两枝植于禁中 - This phrase can be read as a single clause: 'thus, because of the eastern envoy, two branches of the willow tree from Yongfeng were planted in the imperial garden.'
  • 定知此后天文里柳宿光中添两星 - This phrase can be read as a single clause: 'it is certain that after this, in the celestial chart, two stars will be added to the willow constellation.'
  • 自峡沿流赴郡 - This phrase can be read as a single clause: 'from the gorge, following the river to the prefecture.'
  • 繁知一闻居易将过巫山先于神女祠粉壁大署之 - This phrase can be read as a single clause: 'Fan Zhiyi heard that Bai Juyi would pass by Mount Wu, so he first inscribed a large notice on the wall of the goddess temple.'
  • 行到巫山必有诗 - This phrase can be read as a single clause: 'when he reaches Mount Wu, he will certainly write a poem.'
  • 为报高唐神女道速排云雨候清词 - This phrase can be read as a single clause: 'to report to the goddess of Gao Tang, quickly arrange the clouds and rain to await the clear words.'
  • 覩题处畅然 - This phrase can be read as a single clause: 'seeing the inscription, he felt relieved.'
  • 历阳刘郎中禹锡三年理白帝欲作一诗于 - This phrase can be read as a single clause: 'Liuyuxi, the prefect of Liyang, who had been in charge of Baidi for three years, wanted to write a poem.'

  • 又祐题金山寺诗曰影中流见钟声两岸闻虽綦毋濳云塔影挂青汉钟声和白云此句未为佳也 - Sentence comparing Zhang's poem about Jinshan Temple to a line by Qiu Wu, suggesting that Zhang's poem is inferior.

Later, when they met again, Bai was the governor and Li was a county magistrate, and Bai rode a horse while Li used a sedan chair, which seemed disrespectful.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 李嘗謂白為嗫嚅兒 - A metaphorical or pejorative nickname implying hesitation or reticence, used by Li to describe Bai.
  • 聞者皆笑 - Indicates the nickname was widely known and regarded as humorous or ironic.
  • 樂天之名稍減矣 - Implies that Bai's reputation was affected by the nickname.
  • 吾之猘子也 - Metaphorical term for a spirited or aggressive child or follower, referring to *猘子* as a wild or fierce dog.
  • 其鋒不可當 - Means Bai’s literary or poetic talent is formidable and cannot be challenged.
  • 酷吟祐宮詞 - Refers to intense or passionate recitation or composition of palace poems by Yù.
  • 非祐之論 - Means 'not in favor of Yù’s views,' with *非* meaning 'to criticize' or 'not agree with.'
  • 乃為詩二首以髙之 - Refers to composing two poems to elevate or praise someone, with *髙之* as a classical expression meaning to elevate or honor.
  • 誰人得似張公子 - A rhetorical question praising Zhang, implying no one compares to Zhang’s noble son.
  • 千首詩輕萬戸侯 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'a thousand poems worth more than a hundred thousand lords,' with *輕* meaning 'surpass' or 'be more valuable than.'
  • 如何故國三千里 - A poetic way of expressing distance from one’s homeland.
  • 虛唱歌詞滿六宮 - Implies singing without purpose or to no avail, with *虛唱* as the key term.
  • 此為祐得意之語也 - Refers to Yù’s favorite line, with *得意之語* meaning 'the line of which he was most proud.'
  • 欲以茍異於白而曲成於張也 - Means 'wanted to be different from Bai in a hasty or superficial way, and thus to favor Zhang,' with *茍異* implying a superficial or hasty attempt to differentiate.
  • 鼓扇浮嚣 - Refers to inciting or encouraging frivolity and disorder, with *浮嚣* meaning superficial or noisy behavior.
  • 未能以法治之 - Means 'unable to govern or control it with law,' with *法治* referring to rule by law or legal governance.
  • 敷佐於祐耳 - Means 'this is merely a form of support for Yù,' with *敷佐* meaning to assist or support in a formal or indirect way.

  • 李 - Refers to **Li Bai**, one of the most famous poets of the Tang Dynasty.

  • 局不离身 - Means 'the game board was never far from his person.'
  • 泛海遇风船破 - Describes a situation where a ship was wrecked by a storm at sea.

  • 日一夜至岸 - Means 'reached the shore after a day and night.'
  • 两手见骨 - Means 'his hands were so thin that the bones could be seen.'
  • 局终不舍 - Means 'he did not part with the game board until the end.'
  • 骰子亦在口 - Means 'the dice were still in his mouth.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 澹泊无为 - Means 'content with simplicity and non-action,' reflecting a Daoist ideal of living in harmony with nature.
  • 披裘带索 - Literally 'wearing a fur coat and a rope,' describes a simple, rustic lifestyle.
  • 沈浮人间 - Means 'floating and sinking in the world,' suggesting a life that is neither too involved nor too detached from society.
  • 遗以衣服 - Means 'gave him clothes,' indicating an attempt to integrate him into society.
  • 逼为乡正 - Means 'forced him to be a village headman,' showing an attempt to impose social roles on him.
  • 不言而退 - Means 'without saying anything, he left,' indicating his refusal to engage with the imposed role.
  • 逃入山中 - Means 'escaped into the mountains,' emphasizing his retreat from society.
  • 织芒屩置之于路 - Means 'wove sandals and placed them on the road,' indicating his self-sufficiency and sharing with others.
  • 见者皆曰朱居士之屩也 - Means 'those who saw them said, 'These are the sandals of Master Zhu,'' showing his reputation and the respect he earned.
  • 士亷下车深加礼敬 - Means 'Governor Li Xian got off his carriage and deeply respected him,' indicating his high regard for Zhu Taozhi.
  • 召至降阶与语 - Means 'called him over, stepped down from the platform, and spoke with him,' showing his humility and respect.
  • 瞪目便去 - Means 'stared blankly and then left,' indicating Zhu Taozhi's indifference to social norms.
  • 召入赐宴拜谏议大夫赐章服并辞不受 - Means 'invited him in, gave him a banquet, appointed him as a Counselor, and gave him official robes, which he refused,' highlighting his refusal of official recognition.
  • 给米百石绢百疋送还隐居之处 - Means 'gave him a hundred shi of rice and a hundred bolts of silk and sent him back to his place of seclusion,' showing the practical support provided.
  • 天宝之乱 - Refers to the An Lushan Rebellion during the Tang Dynasty.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 性放旷美谈笑 - Describes a person as naturally unrestrained, generous in laughter, and well-liked for their wit. '放旷' means unrestrained or free-spirited; '美谈笑' means skilled in conversation and humor.
  • 特相亲善 - '特' means particularly or especially; '相亲善' means to have a close and friendly relationship.
  • 言论调态 - Refers to the tone, style, and manner of speech.
  • 真可谓风流之士 - A classical phrase meaning 'truly can be called a man of elegance and charm' or 'a paragon of refined and cultivated behavior.'
  • 自号四明狂客 - '自号' means to give oneself a nickname or title; '四明' refers to a region in Zhejiang (modern-day Ningbo area); '狂客' is a poetic or eccentric title, often used for poets or recluses.
  • 醉后属词动成篇卷 - '醉后' means after drinking; '属词' means to compose poetry; '动成篇卷' means he would compose entire poems or volumes on the spot.
  • 文不加防 - '防' here is likely a variant or misspelling of '删' (to edit or revise). Thus, this likely means 'without revision' or 'without editing.'
  • 善草隶书 - '善' means skilled in; '草隶书' refers to cursive and clerical script, two traditional Chinese calligraphy styles.
  • 好事者共传宝之 - '好事者' means those who are fond of collecting or valuing rare things; '共传宝之' means they all passed it down as a treasure.
  • 请为道士归乡舍宅为观 - '请为道士' means to request to become a Taoist monk; '归乡舍宅为观' means to return to his hometown and convert his house into a Taoist temple or monastery.
  • 御制诗以赠行 - '御制' means composed by the emperor; '赠行' means to compose a poem to send off someone on their journey.
  • 有时宰曾招致将以好官命之 - '有时宰' refers to a high official or minister; '招致' means to invite or summon; '将以好官命之' means to appoint him to a good or important position.
  • 四海如今已太平 - A common phrase used to express that the world is now peaceful and stable.
  • 相公何事唤狂生 - '相公' is a respectful title for a high official or minister; '唤狂生' means to call a 'madman' or 'eccentric' — a poetic way of addressing someone who is not conventional.
  • 得道解化去 - '得道' means to attain spiritual enlightenment; '解化去' means to transform or vanish, often used to describe someone who has attained immortality or left the physical world.
  • 陈琡鸿之子也 - '陈琡' is the name of the person; '鸿之子' means the son of Chen Hong.
  • 白傅传长恨词 - '白傅' refers to Bai Juyi (White居易), who was known as 'Bai Fu' (White the Teacher); '长恨词' is 'The Song of Everlasting Sorrow,' a famous poem by Bai Juyi about the Tang emperor Xuanzong and his concubine Yang Guifei.
  • 文格极髙盖良史也 - '文格' means literary style or quality; '极髙' means extremely high; '盖良史也' means he was a good historian or chronicler.
  • 性尤耿介非其人不与之交 - '耿介' means upright and principled; '非其人不与之交' means he would not associate with those he deemed unworthy.
  • 同院有小计姓武亦元衡相国之后 - '同院' means in the same office or department; '小计' may be a title or position; '姓武' means of the surname Wu; '元衡相国' refers to the former prime minister Yuanheng.
  • 盖汾阳之坦牀也 - '汾阳' refers to the historical figure Guo Ziyi, who was known as 'Fenyang' (modern-day Shanxi); '坦牀' is a term that can refer to a person who is a close associate or a sleeping companion, but here it may be a term of endearment or a nickname.
  • 乃心不平之 - '乃' means 'then' or 'thus'; '心不平' means his heart was not at peace or he was not satisfied.
  • 遂挈家居于茅山 - '挈家' means to take one's family; '居于茅山' means to settle in Maoshan, a famous Daoist mountain in Jiangsu.
  • 与妻子隔山而居 - '隔山而居' means to live on the other side of the mountain from one's wife and children.
  • 短褐束縧焚香习禅而已 - '短褐' means a simple, coarse robe; '束縧' refers to tying up one's hair in a simple style, indicating asceticism; '焚香习禅' means to burn incense and practice Zen meditation.
  • 或一年半载与妻子略相面焉 - '或' means sometimes; '一年半载' is a classical way of saying 'one or two years'; '略相面' means to briefly meet or see one's wife and children.
  • 在职之时唯流沟寺长老与之欵接 - '在职之时' means during his time in office; '唯' means only; '流沟寺长老' is the abbot or elder of Liugou Temple; '欵接' means to receive hospitably or to have friendly interaction.
  • 亦具短褐相 - '具短褐' means to wear a simple robe; '相' here may be a misspelling or variant of '相见' (to meet) or '相待' (to treat or receive).

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 于司空頔常令客弹琴其嫂知音聴于下叹曰三分之中一分筝声二分琵琶声无本色韵 - Describes a situation where the sister-in-law of Yu Didi listens to a guest playing the qin and comments on the sound, noting that it contains one part of the zheng, two parts of the pipa, and lacks the pure tone of the qin.
  • 韩臯生知音律尝观弹琴至止息叹曰妙哉稽生之为是也 - Describes Han Gao, who is knowledgeable about music theory, observing someone playing the qin and commenting on the beauty of the performance, attributing it to Ji Sheng.
  • 其当晋魏之际其音主商商为秋声秋也者天揺落肃杀其嵗之晏乎 - Describes the musical characteristics of the period between the Jin and Wei dynasties, noting that the music primarily uses the shang mode, which is associated with the autumn season, characterized by falling leaves and a solemn atmosphere.
  • 晋承金运之声也此所以知魏之季而晋代之也 - Explains that the music of the Jin dynasty reflects the characteristics of the metal element, which is associated with the end of the Wei dynasty and the rise of the Jin dynasty.
  • 慢其商以宫同音是臣夺君之义也此所以知司马氏之簒也 - Describes a situation where the shang string is played with the same pitch as the gong string, symbolizing the usurpation of power by a minister, which is attributed to the Sima family's coup.
  • 司马懿受魏明帝顾托后反有簒夺之心自诛曹爽逆节弥露王陵都督州谋立楚王彪毌丘俭文钦诸诞 - Describes the political situation where Sima Yi, after being entrusted by Emperor Ming of Wei, eventually shows signs of usurpation, leading to the execution of Cao Shuang and the rebellion of Wang Ling and others.
  • 前后相继为州都督咸有匡复魏室之谋皆为懿父子所杀叔夜以州故广陵之地彼四人者皆魏室文武大臣咸散败于广陵故名其曲为广陵散言魏氏防亡自广陵始也 - Describes the historical events where several officials, including Wang Ling, successively serve as the governor of Yangzhou and plot to restore the Wei dynasty, but are all killed by Sima Yi and his sons. Ji Sheng, who is from Yangzhou (Guangling), composes a piece called 'Guangling San' to commemorate these events.
  • 止息者晋虽暴兴终止息于此也其哀愤戚惨痛廹切之音尽在于是永嘉之乱是其应乎 - Explains that 'Guangling San' ends with a sense of finality, reflecting the tragic end of the Wei dynasty and the subsequent chaos of the Yongjia Rebellion.
  • 叔夜撰此贻后代之知音者且避晋祸所以托之神也 - Describes how Ji Sheng composed 'Guangling San' to be passed down to future generations who understand music, and to avoid the political turmoil of the Jin dynasty, he attributed the composition to a deity.
  • 臯之于音可谓至矣 - Concludes that Han Gao is highly knowledgeable about music.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 弹一曲坐客弥加悚敬非寻常之品调 - This long sequence must be read as a single description of the impact of the music on the guests.
  • 山甫遂命酒停各引满数杯 - This sequence describes Shanfu's actions and the guests' responses in a single, continuous flow.

  • 山甫早疑其音韵殆似神工又见王生之说即知古之广陵散或传于世矣 - This sequence describes Shanfu's realization and the significance of the tune.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 大酺 - Grand feast or banquet, often held to celebrate significant events.
  • 命三百里内县令刺史率其声乐来赴阙者 - Imperial command for county magistrates and prefectural governors within a radius of three hundred miles to bring their musical performers to the capital.
  • 或谓令较其胜负而赏罚焉 - Some people said that the emperor would judge the performances and reward or punish accordingly.
  • 河内郡守令乐工数百人于车上皆衣以锦绣伏厢之牛以虎皮及为犀象形状观者骇目 - The governor of He'nan Prefecture ordered hundreds of musicians to perform on carriages adorned with brocade, with cows covered in tiger skins and decorated to resemble rhinoceroses and elephants, which amazed the spectators.
  • 元鲁山遣乐工数十人聨袂歌于蒍于蒍鲁山之文也 - Yuan Lushan sent dozens of musicians to perform a song called 'Yu Wei,' which was written by Yuan Lushan.
  • 其后上谓宰臣曰河内之人其在涂炭乎促命征还而授以散秩 - Later, the emperor told his ministers, 'Are the people of He'nan suffering? I will immediately order them to be recalled and given a low-ranking position.'
  • 毎赐宴设酺防则上御勤政楼金吾及四军兵士未明陈仗盛列旗帜皆被黄金甲衣短后绣袍太常陈乐卫尉张幕后诸蕃酋长就食府县教坊大陈山车旱船寻撞走索丸剑角抵戏马闘鸡又令宫女数百饰以珠翠衣以锦绣自帷中出击雷鼓为破阵乐太平乐上元乐又引大象犀牛入场或拜舞动中音律毎正月望夜又御勤政楼观作乐贵臣戚里官设防楼夜防即遣宫女于楼前歌舞以娱之 - Whenever a grand feast was held, the emperor would personally attend the Qinzhen Tower, with the Gold Guards and the Four Armies setting up their formations before dawn, with flags and banners adorned with golden armor and short-sleeved embroidered robes. The Grand Music Bureau would arrange the music, and the Palace Guards would set up the curtains behind which foreign chieftains would dine. The Imperial Music Bureau would perform grand shows with mountain carts, dry boats, and acrobatic performances, as well as horse races and chicken fights. The emperor would also order hundreds of palace maidens dressed in jewels and brocade to strike thunder drums from behind the curtains, performing 'Battle Array Music,' 'Peace Music,' and 'Lantern Festival Music.' Elephants and rhinoceroses would be brought into the arena, sometimes bowing and dancing in rhythm with the music. On the night of the first full moon of the year, the emperor would again attend the Qinzhen Tower to watch the performances, and the nobles and officials would set up viewing pavilions, sending palace maidens to dance and sing in front of the tower for entertainment.

  • 汉戚夫人 - Refers to the concubine of Emperor Gaozu of Han, known for her beauty and talent.

  • 歌出塞入塞望归之曲 - Songs that express the longing of soldiers and their families for home, often performed during military campaigns.
  • 侍婢数百人 - Indicates the large number of attendants, reflecting the high status and wealth of the concubine.

  • 绿野堂 - Refers to a villa built by Pei Jingong, a prominent statesman, after moving it from its original location.
  • 明皇杂录 - A historical record of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, providing context for the lives of these figures.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 开元中禁中初重木芍药即今牡丹也 - 开元年间宫中开始重视木芍药，也就是今天的牡丹。
  • 得四夲红紫浅红通白者 - 得到了四种颜色的牡丹：红色、紫色、浅红色和白色。
  • 上因移植于兴庆池东沈香亭前 - 皇帝因此将这些牡丹移栽到了兴庆池东边的沈香亭前。
  • 防花方繁开 - 花儿正盛开。
  • 上乘照夜白太眞妃以歩辇从 - 皇帝骑着照夜白马，杨贵妃乘坐步辇跟随。
  • 诏特梨园弟子中尤者得乐十六部 - 下诏特别挑选了梨园弟子中最优秀的十六人演奏音乐。
  • 上曰赏名花对妃子焉用旧乐词为 - 皇帝说：‘欣赏名花，面对妃子，为什么要用旧的歌词呢？’
  • 白欣然承旨犹苦宿酲未解因援笔赋之 - 李白欣然接受命令，但因宿醉未醒，于是提笔作诗。
  • 辞曰云想衣裳花想容春风晓拂露华浓若非羣玉山头见防向瑶台月下逢 - 诗中写道：‘云想衣裳花想容，春风拂晓露华浓。若非群玉山头见，会向瑶台月下逢。’
  • 一枝红艶露凝香云雨巫山枉断肠借问汉宫谁得似可怜飞燕倚新妆 - 另一首诗中写道：‘一枝红艳露凝香，云雨巫山枉断肠。借问汉宫谁得似，可怜飞燕倚新妆。’
  • 名花倾国两相欢长得君王 - 名花和倾国之色相互映衬，赢得了君王的喜爱。

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 开元 - 指唐玄宗李隆基的年号，开元年间是唐朝的鼎盛时期。
  • 禁中 - 指皇宫内部。
  • 木芍药 - 牡丹的古称。
  • 照夜白 - 一种名马，唐代著名骏马之一。
  • 太眞妃 - 即杨贵妃，唐玄宗的宠妃。
  • 梨园弟子 - 指宫廷乐师，因唐玄宗曾在梨园教习音乐而得名。
  • 清平调 - 唐代的一种乐府诗体，通常用于宫廷宴会。
  • 云雨巫山 - 出自《高唐赋》，形容男女欢爱。
  • 飞燕 - 指汉朝的赵飞燕，以美貌著称。

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 带笑防解释春风无限恨 - Highly poetic phrase; likely a miswritten or corrupted version of a classical poetic line or name.

  • 遽以辞进 - Means 'hurriedly presented a poem or words.'
  • 太眞妃持玻瓈七宝盏酌西凉州蒲桃酒 - The Lady Taizhen held a glass made of seven precious materials and poured *Xiliangzhou* *putao* wine. *Xiliangzhou* (西凉州) is a region in the northwest, known for its grape production, and *putao* (葡萄) is the Chinese word for grape.
  • 笑领歌意甚厚 - Smilingly accepted the song’s meaning with great warmth.
  • 上因调玉笛以倚曲 - The Emperor then played the jade flute to accompany the melody.
  • 太真饮罢敛绣巾重拜上 - After drinking, Lady Taizhen folded her embroidered handkerchief and bowed deeply to the Emperor.
  • 上自是顾李翰林尤异于他学士 - From then on, the Emperor regarded Li Bai (the翰林, or Hanlin scholar) with special favor, unlike the other scholars.
  • 异日太真妃重吟前词 - On another day, Lady Taizhen recited the previous verse again.
  • 力士戏曰此为妃子怨李白深入骨髓何反拳拳如是 - Gao Lishi joked, 'This is the Empress’s resentment toward Li Bai, so deep in her bones, yet why does she still show such affection?'
  • 太真因惊曰何翰林学士能辱人如斯 - Lady Taizhen was surprised and said, 'How could the Hanlin scholar be so disrespectful?'
  • 太真颇深然之上尝三欲命李白官卒为宫中所捍而止 - Lady Taizhen was deeply offended, but the Emperor had three times attempted to appoint Li Bai to an official post, but the palace officials prevented it.
  • 出松牕録 - From *Songchuang Lu* (松牕録), a classical Chinese text.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 沉香亭 - The *Chénxiāng Pavilion*, a famous pavilion in the imperial gardens of the Tang Dynasty, associated with Emperor Xuanzong and Yang Guifei.
  • 太真妃 - A title for Yang Guifei, the beloved concubine of Emperor Xuanzong of the Tang Dynasty. *太真* is one of her many titles.
  • 丝竹 - Refers to the traditional Chinese musical instruments, *sī* (stringed instruments like the guqin or pipa) and *zhú* (bamboo instruments like the flute or sheng).
  • 玉笛 - A jade flute, a refined musical instrument often associated with poetry and court music.
  • 西凉州蒲桃酒 - *Xiliangzhou* (西凉州), a region in the northwest, known for its grape production, and *putao* (葡萄) is the Chinese word for grape. This refers to a type of wine.
  • 李翰林 - Li Bai (李白), the famous Tang poet, known as the *Hanlin* scholar (翰林), a title given to scholars in the imperial court.
  • 松牕録 - *Songchuang Lu* (松牕録), a classical Chinese text, possibly a collection of anecdotes or historical notes from the Tang Dynasty.

[2415]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 李舟好事 - Biographical phrase meaning 'Li Zhou, who was fond of good things,' introducing the subject of the passage.
  • 截為笛 - Means 'cut into a flute,' describing the action of making a flute from bamboo.
  • 堅如鐵石 - Simile meaning 'as hard as iron and stone,' emphasizing the durability of the flute.
  • 遺李謩 - Means 'gave to Li Mo,' indicating the transfer of the flute from Li Zhou to Li Mo.
  • 謩吹笛天下第一 - Superlative phrase meaning 'Li Mo was the first flute player in the world,' praising his skill.
  • 月夜泛江 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'rowed on the river at night,' setting the scene for Li Mo's performance.
  • 與同舟人吹 - Means 'played with fellow passengers,' indicating the collaborative nature of the performance.
  • 俄有客於岸呼舟請載 - Narrative phrase meaning 'soon a guest on the shore called for a boat to be taken,' introducing a new character.
  • 既至請笛而吹 - Means 'after arriving, he asked for the flute and played,' describing the guest's request.
  • 甚為精妙 - Means 'very exquisite,' praising the quality of the guest's performance.
  • 山石可裂 - Hyperbolic phrase meaning 'could make mountains and stones crack,' emphasizing the power of the music.
  • 謩平生未嘗見 - Means 'Li Mo had never seen this before,' indicating his surprise.
  • 入破呼吸盤擗 - Technical term meaning 'the rhythm of the *pò* section, where the breath is held and the fingers move rapidly,' describing a specific part of the performance.
  • 應指粉碎 - Means 'crumbled with the touch of the fingers,' emphasizing the intensity of the music.
  • 客散不知所之 - Means 'the guest left and no one knew where he went,' indicating the mysterious departure of the guest.
  • 疑其蛟龍也 - Means 'suspected it was a dragon,' suggesting a supernatural interpretation.
  • 謩嘗秋夜吹笛於洲 - Means 'Li Mo once played the flute at night on the 洲 (a place name),' setting the scene for another performance.
  • 檝載甚隘 - Means 'the boat was very narrow,' describing the setting.
  • 初發調羣動皆息 - Means 'when he first played, all movements ceased,' emphasizing the power of the music.
  • 及數奏防風飒然立至 - Means 'after several plays, a strong wind suddenly arose,' describing the atmospheric effect.
  • 有頃舟人賈客有怨叹悲泣之声 - Means 'soon the boatman and merchants began to weep with sorrow,' indicating the emotional impact of the music.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 李舟 - A historical figure, a friend of Li Mo.
  • 李謩 - A famous flute player during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 洲 - A place name, possibly referring to a specific location in the Yangtze River.
  • 檝載 - Refers to a narrow boat.
  • 防風 - Refers to a strong wind.
  • 舟人 - Refers to the boatman.
  • 賈客 - Refers to merchants.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • Long sentences like '李舟好事嘗得村舍煙竹截為笛堅如鐵石以遺李謩' describe Li Zhou's action of cutting bamboo into a flute and giving it to Li Mo.
  • Sentences like '謩吹笛天下第一月夜泛江與同舟人吹' describe Li Mo's performance on the river at night with fellow passengers.
  • Sentences like '俄有客於岸呼舟請載既至請笛而吹甚為精妙山石可裂' describe the arrival of a guest who plays the flute and the powerful effect of his music.
  • Sentences like '謩平生未嘗見及入破呼吸盤擗應指粉碎客散不知所之' describe Li Mo's surprise and the mysterious departure of the guest.
  • Sentences like '謩嘗秋夜吹笛於洲檝載甚隘初發調羣動皆息及數奏防風飒然立至有頃舟人賈客有怨叹悲泣之声' describe another performance by Li Mo and its emotional impact.

Proper Nouns

  • 李舟 - A historical figure.
  • 李謩 - A famous flute player.
  • 洲 - A place name.
  • 檝載 - A type of boat.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有神之来 - Describes a ghost or spirit appearing, where  (ghost) and 神 (spirit) are used together to emphasize the supernatural nature of the visitor.
  • 皆更赞咏之 - Means 'everyone praises and sings about it,' indicating a collective admiration.
  • 以为钧天之乐不如也 - '钧天之乐' refers to the music of heaven, a term often used in ancient texts to describe celestial or divine music. '不如也' means 'is not as good as.'
  • 独孤生乃无一言防者 - '独孤生' is a proper name, likely referring to a specific individual. '无一言防者' means 'did not say a single word in defense.'
  • 皆怒李生为轻己意甚忿之 - '李生' is another proper name, likely referring to a specific individual. '轻己意甚忿之' means 'they were very angry because he thought they were being light-hearted.'
  • 良久又静思作一曲更加妙绝 - '良久' means 'after a long time,' and '静思作一曲' means 'quietly thought and composed a piece of music.' '更加妙绝' means 'even more wonderful.'
  • 独孤生又无言 - '独孤生' again refers to the same individual, and '无言' means 'did not speak.'
  • 邻居召至者甚惭悔白于众 - '邻居召至者' means 'those who were called by the neighbors,' and '甚惭悔' means 'very ashamed and regretful.' '白于众' means 'admitted to the crowd.'
  • 独孤村落幽处城郭稀至 - '独孤村落' refers to a remote village, and '幽处' means 'secluded.' '城郭稀至' means 'rarely visited by people from the city.'
  • 音乐之率所不通防客同诮责之 - '音乐之' means 'the kind of music,' and '率所不通' means 'generally not familiar with.' '防客同诮责之' means 'the guests criticized him together.'
  • 独孤生不答但防笑而已 - '独孤生不答' means '独孤生 did not answer,' and '但防笑而已' means 'just laughed silently.'
  • 公如是是轻薄为复是好手 - '公如是' means 'if you are like this,' and '轻薄为复是好手' means 'are you being light-hearted or are you a good player?'
  • 独孤生乃徐曰 - '独孤生乃徐曰' means '独孤生 then slowly said.'
  • 公安知仆不防也 - '公安知' means 'how do you know,' and '仆不防也' means 'I am not afraid.'
  • 坐客皆为李生改容谢之 - '坐客皆为李生改容谢之' means 'all the guests changed their expressions and apologized to 李生.'
  • 独孤曰 - '独孤曰' means '独孤 said.'
  • 公试吹凉州至曲终 - '公试吹' means 'please try to play,' and '凉州至曲终' means 'the entire piece of music from Liangzhou.'
  • 独孤生曰 - '独孤生曰' means '独孤生 said.'
  • 李生大骇起拜曰 - '李生大骇' means '李生 was greatly shocked,' and '起拜曰' means 'stood up and bowed, saying.'
  • 又曰第十二叠误入水调足下知之乎 - '又曰' means 'he also said,' and '第十二叠误入水调足下知之乎' means 'the twelfth section mistakenly entered the water melody, do you know about it?'
  • 李生曰 - '李生曰' means '李生 said.'
  • 某顽实不觉 - '某顽' means 'I am stupid,' and '实不觉' means 'really didn't notice.'
  • 独孤生乃取吹之 - '独孤生乃取吹之' means '独孤生 then took the instrument and played it.'
  • 李生更有一笛拂拭以进 - '李生更有一笛' means '李生 had another flute,' and '拂拭以进' means 'wiped it clean and presented it.'
  • 独孤视之曰 - '独孤视之曰' means '独孤 looked at it and said.'
  • 此都不堪取执者粗通耳乃换之 - '此都不堪取' means 'this one is not good enough,' and '执者粗通耳乃换之' means 'the one holding it is just barely competent, so let's change it.'
  • 李生曰 - '李生曰' means '李生 said.'
  • 不敢遂吹声发入云四座震栗 - '不敢遂吹' means 'I dare not play,' and '声发入云四座震栗' means 'the sound rose into the clouds, and everyone present was trembling.'
  • 李生蹙踖不敢动至第十三叠掲示谬误之处敬伏拜及入破笛遂败裂不复终曲 - '李生蹙踖' means '李生 was nervous,' and '不敢动' means 'dared not move.' '至第十三叠掲示谬误之处' means 'by the thirteenth section, it revealed the error,' and '敬伏拜及入破笛遂败裂不复终曲' means 'he respectfully bowed and when it reached the climax, the flute broke and the piece could not be finished.'
  • 李生再拜众皆帖息乃散 - '李生再拜' means '李生 bowed again,' and '众皆帖息乃散' means 'everyone became quiet and then dispersed.'
  • 明旦李生并防客皆往候之至则唯茅舍尚存独孤生不见矣 - '明旦' means 'the next day,' and '李生并防客皆往候之' means '李生 and the guests went to visit him.' '至则唯茅舍尚存' means 'they found only the thatched house still standing,' and '独孤生不见矣' means '独孤生 was not there.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 洞晓音律 - Thoroughly understands music and rhythm.
  • 由之天纵 - Innate or divinely bestowed; the emperor's musical talent is natural and extraordinary.
  • 必造其妙 - Must reach its perfection; indicates mastery or excellence.
  • 随意即成 - Can be created at will; highlights spontaneity and ease in creation.
  • 不立章度 - Does not establish fixed rules or patterns; indicates flexibility and creativity.
  • 取适短长 - Takes what is suitable, whether short or long; suggests adaptability.
  • 应指散声皆中防指 - All sounds respond to the fingers and fit the intended pitch; indicates precision and control in playing.
  • 清浊变转 - Changes between high and low pitches; indicates the ability to modulate between different tones.
  • 律吕呼召 - Calling of the twelve tones of the Chinese musical scale; indicates mastery over the fundamental elements of music.
  • 君臣事物迭相制使 - Relationships between ruler and subject, and various elements, are mutually controlled; suggests a harmonious balance in music.
  • 八音之领袖 - Leader among the eight tones; indicates the羯鼓's importance in music.
  • 诸乐不可为比 - No other musical instruments can be compared to it; emphasizes the羯鼓's superiority.
  • 常云 - Often says; indicates a habitual statement or remark.
  • 诘旦 - Early morning or dawn; suggests the time of day.
  • 巾栉方毕 - Just finished dressing and grooming; indicates the completion of morning routines.
  • 时宿雨始晴 - At the time when the previous night's rain had just cleared up; sets the scene.
  • 景色明丽 - The scenery is bright and beautiful; describes the environment.
  • 对此景物岂可不与他判断之乎 - How could one not judge this scenery?; indicates a rhetorical question.
  • 左右相目 - The people around him looked at each other; suggests a moment of shared understanding.
  • 将命备酒 - Ordered to prepare wine; indicates a command to serve wine.
  • 上旋命之 - The emperor immediately ordered him; indicates a quick decision.
  • 临轩纵击一曲 - Played a piece at the window; indicates a performance.
  • 曲名春光好 - The name of the piece is 'Spring Light is Good'; indicates the title of the music.
  • 神思自得 - The emperor felt a sense of divine inspiration; indicates a state of mind.
  • 及顾柳杏皆已发拆 - When he looked back, the willows and apricot trees had already blossomed; indicates the passage of time.
  • 指而笑 - Pointed and laughed; indicates a reaction.
  • 谓嫔嫱内官 - Told the concubines and court ladies; indicates a statement to others.
  • 此一事不唤我作天公可乎 - How could one not call me the Heavenly Emperor for this?; indicates a rhetorical question.
  • 每至秋空迥激 - Every time the autumn sky is clear and the wind is strong; indicates a specific condition.
  • 即奏之 - Then played it; indicates a performance.
  • 必远风徐来 - The distant wind would gently blow; indicates a natural phenomenon.
  • 庭叶徐下 - Leaves would slowly fall from the trees; indicates a visual effect.
  • 其妙绝入神 - Its excellence is beyond human comprehension; indicates unparalleled skill.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 八音 - Refers to the eight categories of musical instruments in ancient China: metal, stone, silk, bamboo, gourd, hide, earth, and wood.
  • 君臣事物迭相制使 - A metaphorical phrase that reflects the hierarchical and balanced relationships in Chinese society, often applied to music to symbolize harmony and order.
  • 春光好 - The name of a musical piece composed by Emperor Xuanzong, reflecting his love for nature and music.
